2604.25383.title: ML-SAN: Multi-Level Speaker-Adaptive Network for Emotion Recognition in Conversations
2604.25383.paper_summary: To establish empathy with machines, it is essential to fully understand human emotional changes. However, research in multimodal emotion recognition often overlooks one problem: individual expressive traits vary significantly, which means that different people may express emotions differently. In our daily lives, we can see this. When communicating with different people, some express "happiness" through their facial expressions and words, while others may hide their happiness or express it through their actions. Both are expressions of 'happiness,' but such differences in emotional expression are still too difficult for machines to distinguish. Current emotion recognition remains at a 'static' level, using a single recognition model to identify all emotional styles. This "simplification" often affects the recognition results, especially in multi-turn dialogues. To address this problem, this paper introduces a novel Multi-Level Speaker Adaptive Network (ML-SAN), which, specifically, effectively addresses the challenge of speaker identity information confusion. ML-SAN does not simply assign a speaker's ID after recognition; instead, it employs a three-stage adaptive process: First, Input-level Calibration uses Feature-Level Linear Modulation (FiLM) to adjust the raw audio and visual features into a neutral space unrelated to the speaker. Then, Interaction-level Gating re-adjusts the trust level for each modality (e.g., voice or facial features) based on the speaker's identity information. Finally, Output-level Regularization maintains the consistency of speaker features in the latent space. Tests on the MELD and IEMOCAP datasets show that our model (ML-SAN) achieves better results, performs exceptionally well in handling challenging tail sentiment categories, and better addresses the diversity of speakers in real-world scenarios.
2604.25383.paper_summary_zh: 要建立與機器的共鳴，充分理解人類情感變化是至關重要的。<lb/>然而，多模態情感識別的研究常常忽略了一個問題：個體的表達特徵差異顯著，這意味著不同的人可能以不同的方式表達情感。<lb/>在我們的日常生活中，我們可以看到這一點。<lb/>當與不同的人交流時，有些人通過面部表情和言語表達「快樂」，而另一些人則可能隱藏他們的快樂或通過行動表達它。<lb/>這兩者都是「快樂」的表達，但情感表達的這些差異對機器來說仍然太難以區分。<lb/>目前的情感識別仍然停留在「靜態」水平，使用單一的識別模型來識別所有情感風格。<lb/>這種「簡化」常常影響識別結果，特別是在多輪對話中。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，本文介紹了一種新穎的多層說話者自適應網絡（ML-SAN），它特別有效地解決了說話者身份信息混淆的挑戰。<lb/>ML-SAN並不僅僅是在識別後分配說話者的ID；相反，它採用三階段的自適應過程：首先，輸入級別校準使用特徵級別線性調制（FiLM）將原始音頻和視覺特徵調整到與說話者無關的中性空間。<lb/>然後，交互級別閘門根據說話者的身份信息重新調整每種模態（例如，聲音或面部特徵）的信任水平。<lb/>最後，輸出級別正則化保持潛在空間中說話者特徵的一致性。<lb/>在MELD和IEMOCAP數據集上的測試顯示，我們的模型（ML-SAN）取得了更好的結果，在處理挑戰性的尾部情感類別方面表現出色，並更好地應對現實場景中說話者的多樣性。
2604.25383.author: Kexue Wang et.al.
2604.25383.authors: Kexue Wang, Yinfeng Yu, Liejun Wang
